content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

February 25, 2005

Marguerite Lawry, Vice President
Bethel Island Municipal Improvement District
P.O. Box 429
Bethel Island, CA 94511-0429

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-05-012

Dear Ms. Lawry:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act")¹ on your own behalf and that of fellow board member, Robert Cameron. Your request, received by the Commission via facsimile transmission on February 3, 2005, asked whether the "public generally" exception applied to you and Boardmember Cameron as members of the Bethel Island Municipal Improvement District ("BIMID"), allowing each of you to participate in the discussion and vote on matters before BIMID with respect to the planned DUC housing development.

On the same day we received your request, the district board, including both you and Boardmember Cameron, unanimously voted to approve the district's taking on the maintenance responsibilities for building levees inside the island, which will provide lagoons for homes that will be built as part of the development.

Please be advised that the Commission will not provide advice with respect to past conduct, such as voting that has already taken place. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A), copy enclosed.) As the vote to maintain the levees is inextricably interrelated with the remaining issues concerning the development, the Commission is unable to provide advice on this matter.

___________________________

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations. All statutory references herein are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. All regulatory references herein are to Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations unless otherwise indicated.

File No. W-05-012
Page No. 2

If you believe that the remaining decisions with respect to the proposed
development involve different issues that were not impacted by the previous vote, you
may write and ask for reconsideration based on those factors.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: William J. Lenkeit
Counsel, Legal Division

Enclosure
WJL:jg
I:\AdviceLtrs W-05-012

sections.question_synthetic: Do the public generally exception provisions of the Political Reform Act allow board members to participate in discussions and votes regarding a housing development project affecting their district?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide advice because the requestor has already voted on a matter inextricably interrelated with the development issues in question, and the Commission does not provide advice on past conduct.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: old; LLM: This is a declination letter. The document uses 'W-05-012' file designation. The FPPC declined to provide the requested advice based on regulation 18329(b)(8)(A) prohibiting advice on past conduct. The letter suggests reconsideration may be possible for remaining issues that are separate from the prior vote.

As the vote to maintain the levees is inextricably interrelated with the remaining issues concerning the development, the Commission is unable to provide advice on this matter.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to provide advice to board members of the Bethel Island Municipal Improvement District regarding whether they could participate in votes on a housing development project, because they had already voted on a related matter and the Commission does not advise on past conduct.
